title: Coding Agents Usage
label: How are AI-powered coding tools used in your engineering teams?
context: Coding agent adoption is the strongest leading indicator of how quickly a team can absorb broader AI capability.

options.label: Not used
options.context: No team members use AI coding tools

options.label: ChatGPT / Claude manually
options.context: Copy-paste prompts, no IDE integration

options.label: IDE extensions
options.context: GitHub Copilot, Cursor, Codeium

options.label: Terminal-based agents
options.context: Claude Code, Codex, Aider in workflow

options.label: Custom internal agents
options.context: Self-built tools on top of LLM APIs

title: LLM Foundational Knowledge
label: What is your team's understanding of how LLMs actually work?
context: Foundational understanding determines whether the team can debug, evaluate, and choose the right model for a job.

options.label: Beginner
options.context: Basic ChatGPT usage; no mental model

options.label: Intermediate
options.context: Understand tokens, context windows, limitations

options.label: Working knowledge
options.context: Comfortable with prompting patterns and tradeoffs

options.label: Advanced
options.context: Embeddings, RAG, fine-tuning concepts understood

options.label: Expert
options.context: Built and shipped production LLM systems

title: Automated LLM Usage
label: Do you use LLMs in background processes or only in interactive chat?
context: The shift from interactive to automated LLM use is where most of the ROI shows up.

options.label: Manual only
options.context: All LLM use is human-in-the-loop chat

options.label: Limited automation
options.context: POC or pilot of one batch job

options.label: Some processes automated
options.context: A few production jobs run on a schedule

options.label: Many processes automated
options.context: LLMs embedded across multiple workflows

options.label: Fully systematized
options.context: Most repeatable cognitive work is LLM-mediated

title: MCP / Skills / Plugin Expertise
label: How comfortable is your team with custom tools, plugins, or MCP servers?
context: Tool/plugin authorship is where AI stops being a chat product and becomes infrastructure.

options.label: Unfamiliar with the concept
options.context: Have not heard of MCP / plugins

options.label: Aware but not using
options.context: Know it exists; have not tried

options.label: Using off-the-shelf
options.context: Using public plugins and integrations

options.label: Configured / customized
options.context: Tuned existing tools for our workflow

options.label: Built our own
options.context: Authored MCP servers / skills in-house

title: Data Infrastructure
label: How mature is your data infrastructure for analytics and ML?
context: Data infrastructure is the floor for what AI work is even possible.

options.label: No data warehouse
options.context: No structured data pipeline

options.label: Basic logging
options.context: Limited ability to query for analytics

options.label: Data warehouse exists
options.context: Analytics database with ETL for key datasets

options.label: Comprehensive infrastructure
options.context: Automated pipelines, analytics tools in use

options.label: ML-ready platform
options.context: Feature store, real-time processing, ML datasets

title: Team Capabilities
label: What is your team's current ML / data science / analytics experience?
context: Team expertise determines training needs, hiring urgency, and realistic timelines.

options.label: No ML expertise
options.context: No team members with ML / DS background

options.label: Basic analytics
options.context: 1-2 people with SQL / Python / Excel skills

options.label: Multiple data people
options.context: 3+ with data background; basic ML familiarity

options.label: Junior data scientist
options.context: Dedicated analyst or junior DS on team

options.label: Experienced ML team
options.context: Production ML deployments under their belt

title: AI Strategy & Leadership
label: How committed is leadership to AI as a strategic priority?
context: Leadership commitment determines budget, headcount, and how patient the org will be with the J-curve.

options.label: Not a priority
options.context: No budget or plan for AI initiatives

options.label: Interest only
options.context: Curiosity, no formal strategy

options.label: Early strategy
options.context: Budget allocated, strategy in development

options.label: Clear roadmap
options.context: Dedicated resources and team ownership

options.label: Central to strategy
options.context: Significant investment, C-level accountability

title: Process Automation Maturity
label: How much of your day-to-day work is already automated (any kind)?
context: Existing automation muscle predicts how fast AI automation can be layered on.

options.label: Mostly manual
options.context: Few or no automated workflows

options.label: Some scripts
options.context: Ad-hoc scripts run by individuals

options.label: Standard SaaS automations
options.context: Zapier / n8n / Make in regular use

options.label: Custom workflows
options.context: Internal tools and orchestration in production

options.label: Automation-first culture
options.context: New process is automated by default
